GPO Box: 21363, Old Baneshwor, Kathmandu, Nepal Tel: +977-1-5917841 Web: www.cecnepal.com.np Email: cecpost@gmail.com info@cecnepal.com.np Clean Energy for Generations... Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. (CEC), established in 2006, stands as a leading national consultancy firm with a sharp focus on planning, feasibility studies, detailed engineering design, construction monitoring, and commissioning of hydropower and infrastructure projects. We actively confront the evolving challenges faced by hydropower developers and investors at all project stages, turning these challenges into opportunities for impactful solutions. Our team combines extensive expertise and insights to deliver sustainable outcomes that add significant value to hydropower initiatives, water resource development, geology and tunneling, river management, and other critical infrastructure projects. Rationale of Hydropower Development in Nepal Nepali rivers have the potential to generate about 83,000 megawatts of hydropower, with around 42,000 megawatts deemed financially viable. However, only about 6.7% of this potential has been utilized, translating to approximately 2,800 megawatts. The current national average energy demand is 2,038 megawatts, peaking at 2,380 megawatts. The Nepal Electricity Authority produces approximately 23% of the demand through hydropower, while independent power producers (IPPs) cover 32%. This leaves about 41% of the energy needed in the lean season to be imported. Presently, 78% of energy consumption relies on fuel wood, with only 2% coming from electricity. To enhance hydropower development, the government has enacted several policies, including the Development Policy, Electricity Act, and Water Resources Act of 1992. It aims to generate 10,000 megawatts of hydropower in the next ten years and has secured power purchase agreements for viable projects. The total investment required is around $11 billion, covering production and national transmission line expansion. International interest in investing in Nepal's hydropower sector is growing, and there's a push to export surplus energy to India and Bangladesh. Currently, about 3,000 megawatts of hydropower projects are under construction, with an additional 7,500 megawatts in the planning phase. Most are run-of-the-river projects, generating only 30% of their capacity during low-flow seasons, leading to electricity shortages in the dry season. With a population growth rate of 2.3%, rapid urbanization, and an increasing demand for electric vehicles and industries, electricity demand is expected to rise significantly. Balancing supply and demand remain a challenge due to the lack of large-scale storage and pumped-storage projects

\ Main Motto and Objectives of CEC The primary goal of our company is to promote clean and green energy generation for sustainable development. We provide services to national and international clients interested in clean and green energy initiatives. We provide the following services:   Identification of hydropower projects, including desk studies, topographic surveys, field investigations, feasibility studies, detailed engineering design, due diligence studies, initial environmental evaluations, environmental impact assessments, and project reviews for development.   Physical modeling of river structures, such as headworks, and design optimization.   Troubleshooting studies and problem-solving for operational hydropower projects.   Conducting geophysical surveys, such as Electrical Resistivity Tomography (ERT), Seismic Refraction Tomography (SRT), Multi-Channel Analysis of Surface Waves (MASW), etc., and geotechnical investigations, including drilling, soil, and sediment surveys for various project types.   Troubleshooting in the design, construction, and operation of underground structures, such as tunnels and caverns.   Discharge and sediment measurement, along with detailed hydrological studies. 7. Conducting pre-feasibility and feasibility studies, as well as detailed surveys, investigations, design, and construction supervision for transmission lines and power evacuation studies. 8. Providing detailed design and support for factories and clients regarding hydromechanical components.   Assisting clients in selecting and purchasing electromechanical equipment from international markets.   Preparing tender and bid documents for clean and green energy-related projects.   Creating bankable documents and other supporting materials for banks and financial institutions to finance clean and green energy development projects.   Monitoring, supervising, and verifying construction activities according to the guidelines of banks and financial institutions.   Preparing construction documents and project completion reports following the requirements of various financial institutions.   Providing advisory services to banks and financial institutions regarding due diligence, monitoring, and supervision of financed projects.   Conducting property evaluations for hydropower and clean energy-related industries.   Studying drinking water supply, irrigation, river training, and other water-related projects.   Researching national, provincial, and local plans and policies related to clean and green energy development and management.   Conducting scientific and technical programs addressing water and energy-related issues.   Offering training and support for the sustainable operation and management of hydropower projects.   Developing cooperation and joint ventures with foreign companies/individuals for studying clean energy and river management projects.   Conducting research on the development of sustainable renewable energy resources and providing appropriate and innovative solutions to related challenges.   Acting as an arbitrator and adjudicator to resolve conflicts during the construction activities of various projects.

Er. Mani Raj Dahal is a prominent figure in the hydropower design sector in Nepal. He holds a Master's Degree in Hydraulic Engineering and River Basin Development. Since 2006, he has been working at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. in the role of Chief Executive Engineer and Hydraulic Engineer. To date, he has designed approximately 60 hydropower projects, with more projects currently underway for feasibility studies and detailed designs. Currently, Er. Dahal serves as the Executive Chairman, Chief Engineer, and Hydraulic Expert at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. Er. Santosh Dhakal holds a Master s degree in Electrical Engineering. He has been working at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. since mid-2006 as an Electrical Engineer and EM Expert. During his tenure at Clean Energy Consultants, the company has designed approximately 60 hydropower projects to date, and several more projects are currently undergoing feasibility studies and detailed design. He is also currently working with Hydro Tasmania in Australia. Currently, he is board of Director at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. Er. Kosa Raj Chapagain holds a Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering. Since mid-2011, he has been employed at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. as a Senior Civil Engineer and Project Manager. In this role, he has contributed his expertise in feasibility studies, detailed design, and project monitoring for all projects undertaken by the company. Currently, he is board of Director at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. Mr. Narayan Poudel is a Charted Accountant with extensive experience in his field. He has served as Finance Director for Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. and Aayu Entertainment Pvt. Ltd., and as Chairman of Finman Services Pvt. Ltd. At Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd., he is involved in preparing feasibility reports and conducting financial analyses for various hydropower projects, along with providing overall financial assessments for these projects. Additionally, he offers financial and management consultancy services. Dr. Chhatra Bahadur Basnet is a rock engineering expert who has been working in the Clean Energy Consultants from the early stage of the company establishment. He has completed PhD degree in Rock Engineering in late 2018 and MSc in Hydropower Development in 2013 from the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Trondheim Norway. With over 15 years of experience in the research, design, and implementation of hydropower projects in the Himalayan geological conditions, he has authored numerous scientific papers published in internationally recognized journals and conference proceedings. Dr. Basnet also has experience working with NEA Engineering Company Ltd. Currently, Dr. Basnet is the director cum. Chief Executive Officer cum Geotechnical/Tunnel Expert of Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd. Dr. Bishnu Raj Baral is a hydraulic and sediment engineer with expertise in river hydraulics, sediment transport, river morphology, and hydropower design. He has five years of research experience in the UK, Italy, and the Netherlands, focusing on the hydrodynamics and morphodynamics of rivers, alongside 15 years of consulting experiences in hydropower projects. His work spans planning, feasibility design, detailed design, physical modelling, numerical modelling, and construction monitoring and supervision. He was also a founding member of the Policy and Planning Commission of Gandaki Province, where he helped develop government policies and programs. Currently, he is the Technical Director and hydraulics and sediment expert at Clean Energy Consultants Pvt. Ltd.

Dr. Krishna Kanta Panthi (Independent International Expert in CEC on Rock Engineering and Tunneling) is a Professor in Geological Engineering at the Department of Geoscience and Petroleum, Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Trondheim Norway. He holds the degree of PhD (Dr.Ing.) in Rock Engineering, MSc in Hydropower Development and MSc in Tunneling. He has over 30 years of experience in research, design and implementation of rock engineering, tunneling, hydropower, mining and slope engineering projects. He is the author of over 70 scientific articles and book chapters published in internationally recognized journals, publication canals and conference proceedings. CEC has an agreement with NTNU to receive the expert input on the hydropower projects and to conduct combined research on the rock engineering and tunneling. Dr. Ananta Prasad Gajurel (Independent Expert in CEC on Geology) has lectured at Tribhuvan University for more than 20 years and has assisted with field geoscience courses for Nepali and international students. He has a PhD in sedimentology and geochemistry from Universit  Joseph Fourier in France and was a Fulbright Scholar at Cornell University in 2012 13. His research interests include using sedimentology and geochemistry to better understand climate history and past depositional environments. He is active in GPS/GNSS research of ground surface movement and geohazards education and mitigation. Recently, he has been part of a National Geographic team studying climate change and other environmental factors in the Everest region of Nepal. Mr. Ghan Bahadur Shrestha holds a Master's Degree in Geology. He has worked on several major projects in Nepal, including the Nalsing Gad Hydropower Project (410 MW), Melamchi Water Supply Project, Kabeli-3 Hydropower Project (49 MW), and Upper Chameliya Hydropower Project (40 MW). Mr. Shrestha has extensive knowledge in Geological Studies, Geophysical Surveys, Remote Sensing, and GIS. He is responsible for geological mapping, conducting geophysical surveys, supervising drilling operations, sampling soil and rock, performing laboratory testing, and analyzing results. Additionally, he analyzes radar images and aerial photographs to assist in planning, organizing, and conducting field and site investigations. Mr. Babu Krishna Bhandari holds a Master s Degree in Geological Engineering. His areas of expertise include rock mechanics, soil mechanics, advanced geotechnics, GIS, remote sensing, image interpretation, GIS data analysis, and geomorphology. He has worked on major hydropower projects in Nepal, such as the Upper Tamakoshi Hydroelectric Project (456 MW) and the Super Dordi Hydropower Project-Kha (54 MW). In his role, Mr. Bhandari acts as a manager, supervisor, strategist, communicator, organizer, and goal setter for various projects. His responsibilities include ensuring quality control for all tasks and activities involved in the projects, conducting detailed design work, and providing input for project documentation. This also includes assessing tenders and selecting contractors, among other duties.

Key Manpower and Resource Persons The CEC team consists of highly motivated in-house experts and officers essential for various stages of hydropower study and development. Additionally, CEC has a pool of experts who are contracted on a project-by-project basis, depending on the specific needs of each project. We also engage international specialists for certain tasks. Geophysical and Geotechnical Investigations ABEM Terraloc Pro 2 NEAR SURFACE SEISMICS The ABEM Terraloc Pro 2 is a versatile seismograph that offers a responsive and simplified user interface, to help bring efficiency to a wide range of seismic applications. Seismic solutions are typically used in civil engineering and infrastructure work to determine the properties of the subsurface of the earth, including depth to bedrock, bedrock quality, soil stability studies, finding fractures and weak zones, and geological mapping. The ABEM Terraloc Pro 2 can be configured for all of these applications, and more. Typically, the seismograph is triggered by ground vibrations created by a controlled energy source. By recording the time it takes for the seismic waves to reach geophones connected to the seismograph, it is possible to estimate the depth and properties of subsurface features. ABEM Terrameter LS 2 RESISTIVITY AND IP IMAGING Performance through scalability: Flexible, easy to use and boasting a number of innovative features the ABEM Terrameter LS 2 is the ideal partner for geotechnical, groundwater, mineral or environmental surveys and research work. Resistivity/IP surveying is a versatile geophysical method suitable for a broad range of applications and environments such as groundwater prospecting, mineral exploration, geological mapping, and geotechnical investigations. System design can vary depending on the application. The ABEM Terrameter LS 2 is available in many different configurations to perfectly match your requirements. The instrument benefits from an innovative hardware licensing system, making it scalable and easy to upgrade. Every instrument comes preinstalled with all hardware modules and software features but depending on product package not all need be activated. Product packages range from basic to advanced. Upgrades are made by downloading a license code from the internet or via USB. The instrument is a standalone solution featuring built-in measurement channels, high power current transmitter, electrode selector, computer, and graphical user interface. Surveying techniques range from 1D to 4D measurements. The robust and rugged aluminum casing meets IEC IP66 classification and allows for use in the harshest of conditions. The measurement channels have an improved filter design for enhanced IP performance. Using the 100% duty cycle, data collection will be almost twice as fast and have twice the signal

Core drilling works AIMS to noise ratio compared to the conventional IP method (50 % duty cycle) using the same settings. By utilizing processing packages such as Aarhus Workbench from Aarhus GeoSoftware, it is possible to extract spectral information from time-domain IP data. Future Direction Nepal has abundant hydro potential which is yet to be harnessed for meeting energy demand for the development of our nation. There is bright avenue of Hydropower development in Nepal and we are hopeful from the new developments and peace process in the country after a long political conflict. We are here for the service of the genuine developers of the industry and are always ready to work with various stakeholders who are keen for hydropower and renewable energy related project development. We provide the best quality service in Nepal and abroad. We are keen to build prosperous Nepal through sustainable development of hydropower and other clean and green energy related projects. We believe that economic Development of Nepal is only possible through integrated and sustainable development of our water resources for mutual benefits not only within the geographic regions inside the country but also with our neighbors.
